1	server:
2	  port: 8086
3	
4	spring:
5	  application:
6	    name: ingestion
7	
8	  redis:
9	    # host and port will be overridden by Testcontainers automatically
10	    host: localhost
11	    port: 6379
12	    timeout: 15000
13	
14	    # Redisson-specific configuration
15	    redisson:
16	      # Connection pool settings
17	      #
18	      # Maximum number of connections in Redis command connection pool.
19	      # Redisson doesn't distinguish max-idle, only cares about: maximum pool size and minimum keep-alive connections.
20	      #
21	      # Connection pool optimized for 24 stream consumers + business requests
22	      connection-pool-size: 96
23	
24	      # Minimum number of idle connections to retain in the pool.
25	      # Even if Redis isn't accessed for some time, keep at least this many connections ready.
26	      # Helps avoid "cold start latency" — no need to establish a new connection when requests resume.
27	      #
28	      # High QPS scenario, pre-warm 24 connections.
29	      # Keep a batch of connections resident in the pool, aligned with consumer count.
30	      connection-minimum-idle-size: 24
31	
32	      # Lettuce shares Pub/Sub and regular connections in the same pool (or a few independent).
33	      # But Redisson is different.
34	      #
35	      # Project currently doesn't use Pub/Sub.
36	      subscription-connection-pool-size: 8
37	      subscription-connection-minimum-idle-size: 2
38	
39	      # Redisson thread model:
40	      #      nettyThreads:	IO threads
41	      #      threads:       	Redisson workers (locks/callbacks/scheduling)
42	      # These two thread pools are global, not per-bean
43	      #
44	      # Recommended empirical values:
45	      #      nettyThreads ≈ CPU * 2
46	      #      threads ≈ CPU
47	      netty-threads: 16
48	      threads: 8
49	
50	      # Retry settings
51	      retry-attempts: 5
52	      retry-interval: 1000
53	
54	  datasource:
55	    url: jdbc:mariadb://localhost/bsc?localSocket=/var/lib/mysql/mysql.sock&zeroDateTimeBehavior=convertToNull&useUnicode=true&characterEncoding=UTF-8&CharSet=UTF-8
56	    username: root
57	    password: ""
58	    driver-class-name: org.mariadb.jdbc.Driver
59	
60	  jpa:
61	    hibernate:
62	      ddl-auto: validate
63	      naming:
64	        physical-strategy: org.hibernate.boot.model.naming.PhysicalNamingStrategyStandardImpl
65	    show-sql: false
66	    database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.MariaDBDialect
67	
68	logging:
69	  config: classpath:logback-spring.xml
70	
71	
72	
73	# =====================================================
74	# NAC Stream consumer & producer configuration (used by RedisStreamConsumerBase)
75	# =====================================================
76	nac:
77	  stream:
78	    max-length: 200000            # Maximum number of messages to retain in stream
79	    trim-enabled: true
80	    trim-every-n-writes: 1000
81	
82	    internal:
83	      poll-timeout-ms: 1000             # to avoid busy poll
84	      message-batch-size-per-poll: 20   # Too large batches will increase p95/max (worse tail latency).
85	      redis-consumer-count: 16
86	      worker-thread-pool-size: 32
87	    external:
88	      poll-timeout-ms: 1000             # to avoid busy poll
89	      message-batch-size-per-poll: 50
90	      redis-consumer-count: 8
91	      worker-thread-pool-size: 8
92	
93	    consume-max-retry: 3              # Max retry attempts on message consumption failure
94	    create-group-include-history: true  # Include historical messages when creating group during tests
95	    pending:
96	      idle-threshold-ms: 10000        # Consider stuck if not ACKed within 10 seconds
97	      scan-interval-ms: 5000          # Scan pending messages every 5 seconds
98	      claim-batch-size: 10            # Number of messages to claim per XPENDING batch
99	
100	
101	# =====================================================
102	# Ingestion Controller Reactive Netty Config
103	# =====================================================
104	ingestion:
105	    recovery:
106	      enabled: true
107	      stale-threshold-seconds: 80
108	      fixed-delay-ms: 30000
109	      batch-size: 2000
110	      rate-limit-per-second: 100
111	      async:
112	        core-size: 4
113	        max-size: 16
114	        queue-capacity: 100
115	        keep-alive-seconds: 60
116	    reactive:
117	      thread-pool:
118	        core-size: 30
119	        max-size: 300
120	        queue-capacity: 10000
121	        keep-alive-seconds: 60
122	
123	# =====================================================
124	# Metrics
125	# =====================================================
126	management:
127	  endpoints:
128	    web:
129	      exposure:
130	        # /metrics is for: JVM / thread pools / custom metrics
131	        include: health,info,prometheus,metrics
132	  metrics:
133	    distribution:
134	      # Only enable histogram for lock-hold metric (not global),
135	      # so we can compute p95/p99 from *_bucket while keeping overhead low.
136	      percentiles-histogram:
137	        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: true
138	      # Histogram bucket lower bound for fine-grained range.
139	      # Values below 1ms are still counted (not dropped), but won't be over-segmented.
140	      minimum-expected-value:
141	        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: 1ms
142	      # Histogram bucket upper bound for fine-grained range.
143	      # Values above 60s are still counted (not dropped), and fall into top bucket(s)
144	      # instead of creating more detailed buckets at very large durations.
145	      maximum-expected-value:
146	        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: 60s
147	
148	# =====================================================
149	# Policy Reprocess Job Configuration
150	# =====================================================
151	policy:
152	  reprocess:
153	    # 1. producer side
154	    mark-init-batch-size: 1000
155	
156	    # 2. cron job side
157	    scan-interval-ms: 30000
158	    scan-batch-size: 200
159	    max-candidates-per-scan: 2000
160	    scan-lock-ttl-seconds: 900
161	    rate-limit-per-second: 100
162	
163	    # listener thread pool: handles PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent trigger
164	    listener:
165	      core-size: 1
166	      max-size: 2
167	      queue-capacity: 100
168	      keep-alive-seconds: 60
169	
170	    # job thread pool: executes per-entity reprocess tasks inside each scan batch
171	    job:
172	      core-size: 8
173	      max-size: 16
174	      queue-capacity: 32
175	      keep-alive-seconds: 60
176

32	logging:
33	  level:
34	    # Suppress noisy Lettuce reconnect warnings during application shutdown / test teardown.
35	    # When Redis (e.g. Testcontainers) is stopped, Netty/Lettuce will log ConnectionWatchdog WARN
36	    # due to OS-level socket reset (ECONNRESET / connection closed).
37	    #
38	    # These logs are NOT application errors and do not indicate message loss or processing failure.
39	    # They only occur during shutdown and cannot be fully avoided at code level.
40	    #
41	    # Set to OFF to keep test logs clean and focused on real failures.
42	    io.lettuce.core.protocol.ConnectionWatchdog: OFF
43	
44	    # Extra safeguard: disable all protocol-level reconnect logs from Lettuce.
45	    # This prevents future protocol classes from reintroducing the same shutdown noise.
46	    io.lettuce.core.protocol: OFF
47

1	package com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.config;
2	
3	import io.micrometer.core.instrument.Gauge;
4	import io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry;
5	import io.micrometer.core.instrument.binder.jvm.ExecutorServiceMetrics;
6	
7	import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;
8	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
9	import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
10	
11	import java.util.concurrent.*;
12	
13	@Configuration
14	public class NettyOffLoadThreadPoolConfig {
15	
16	    @Value("${ingestion.reactive.thread-pool.core-size:30}")
17	    private int coreSize;
18	
19	    @Value("${ingestion.reactive.thread-pool.max-size:300}")
20	    private int maxSize;
21	
22	    @Value("${ingestion.reactive.thread-pool.queue-capacity:10000}")
23	    private int queueCapacity;
24	
25	    @Value("${ingestion.reactive.thread-pool.keep-alive-seconds:60}")
26	    private int keepAliveSeconds;
27	
28	    @Bean("nettyOffloadExecutor")
29	    public ExecutorService nettyOffloadExecutor(MeterRegistry registry) {
30	        ThreadPoolExecutor raw = new ThreadPoolExecutor(
31	                coreSize,
32	                maxSize,
33	                keepAliveSeconds, TimeUnit.SECONDS,
34	                new LinkedBlockingQueue<>(queueCapacity),
35	                new NamedThreadFactory("netty-offload"),
36	                new ThreadPoolExecutor.CallerRunsPolicy()
37	        );
38	
39	        // Bind to Micrometer: so that executor_* metrics are visible in /actuator/prometheus*
40	        ExecutorServiceMetrics.monitor(registry, raw, "netty_offload");
41	
42	        // active: the number of threads currently executing tasks (inside run())
43	        //         If active ≈ pool size: the thread pool is nearly fully utilized.
44	        //         If active = pool size for a long time: there is a bottleneck in CPU / I/O / Redis / DB.
45	        //         If active is much smaller than the pool size:
46	        Gauge.builder("netty_offload_active_threads", raw, ThreadPoolExecutor::getActiveCount)
47	                .description("Active threads in nettyOffloadExecutor pool")
48	                .register(registry);
49	
50	        // pool size: the number of threads that have been created and are not yet destroyed
51	        Gauge.builder("netty_offload_pool_size_threads", raw, ThreadPoolExecutor::getPoolSize)
52	                .description("Current number of threads in the pool")
53	                .register(registry);
54	
55	        // corePoolSize: the minimum number of threads that are kept alive in the thread pool
56	        Gauge.builder("netty_offload_pool_core_threads", raw, ThreadPoolExecutor::getCorePoolSize)
57	                .description("Core number of threads for the pool")
58	                .register(registry);
59	
60	        // max size: the maximum number of threads allowed in the thread pool (a hard limit)
61	        Gauge.builder("netty_offload_pool_max_threads", raw, ThreadPoolExecutor::getMaximumPoolSize)
62	                .description("Maximum allowed number of threads in the pool")
63	                .register(registry);
64	
65	        // queue: the number of tasks that have been submitted to the thread pool
66	        //        but have not yet been picked up by any thread.
67	        //
68	        //        If queue = 0: the thread pool is keeping up completely
69	        //        If queue keeps growing: consumption speed < production speed
70	        //        If queue stays large for a long time: tasks are “buffering” inside the JVM
71	        Gauge.builder("netty_offload_queue_size_tasks", raw, e -> e.getQueue().size())
72	                .description("Approximate number of tasks in the queue")
73	                .register(registry);
74

file.content:
server:
  port: 8086

spring:
  application:
    name: ingestion

  redis:
    # host and port will be overridden by Testcontainers automatically
    host: localhost
    port: 6379
    timeout: 15000

    # Redisson-specific configuration
    redisson:
      # Connection pool settings
      #
      # Maximum number of connections in Redis command connection pool.
      # Redisson doesn't distinguish max-idle, only cares about: maximum pool size and minimum keep-alive connections.
      #
      # Connection pool optimized for 24 stream consumers + business requests
      connection-pool-size: 96

      # Minimum number of idle connections to retain in the pool.
      # Even if Redis isn't accessed for some time, keep at least this many connections ready.
      # Helps avoid "cold start latency" — no need to establish a new connection when requests resume.
      #
      # High QPS scenario, pre-warm 24 connections.
      # Keep a batch of connections resident in the pool, aligned with consumer count.
      connection-minimum-idle-size: 24

      # Lettuce shares Pub/Sub and regular connections in the same pool (or a few independent).
      # But Redisson is different.
      #
      # Project currently doesn't use Pub/Sub.
      subscription-connection-pool-size: 8
      subscription-connection-minimum-idle-size: 2

      # Redisson thread model:
      #      nettyThreads:	IO threads
      #      threads:       	Redisson workers (locks/callbacks/scheduling)
      # These two thread pools are global, not per-bean
      #
      # Recommended empirical values:
      #      nettyThreads ≈ CPU * 2
      #      threads ≈ CPU
      netty-threads: 16
      threads: 8

      # Retry settings
      retry-attempts: 5
      retry-interval: 1000

  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mariadb://localhost/bsc?localSocket=/var/lib/mysql/mysql.sock&zeroDateTimeBehavior=convertToNull&useUnicode=true&characterEncoding=UTF-8&CharSet=UTF-8
    username: root
    password: ""
    driver-class-name: org.mariadb.jdbc.Driver

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
      naming:
        physical-strategy: org.hibernate.boot.model.naming.PhysicalNamingStrategyStandardImpl
    show-sql: false
    database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.MariaDBDialect

logging:
  config: classpath:logback-spring.xml



# =====================================================
# NAC Stream consumer & producer configuration (used by RedisStreamConsumerBase)
# =====================================================
nac:
  stream:
    max-length: 200000            # Maximum number of messages to retain in stream
    trim-enabled: true
    trim-every-n-writes: 1000

    internal:
      poll-timeout-ms: 1000             # to avoid busy poll
      message-batch-size-per-poll: 20   # Too large batches will increase p95/max (worse tail latency).
      redis-consumer-count: 16
      worker-thread-pool-size: 32
    external:
      poll-timeout-ms: 1000             # to avoid busy poll
      message-batch-size-per-poll: 50
      redis-consumer-count: 8
      worker-thread-pool-size: 8

    consume-max-retry: 3              # Max retry attempts on message consumption failure
    create-group-include-history: true  # Include historical messages when creating group during tests
    pending:
      idle-threshold-ms: 10000        # Consider stuck if not ACKed within 10 seconds
      scan-interval-ms: 5000          # Scan pending messages every 5 seconds
      claim-batch-size: 10            # Number of messages to claim per XPENDING batch


# =====================================================
# Ingestion Controller Reactive Netty Config
# =====================================================
ingestion:
    recovery:
      enabled: true
      stale-threshold-seconds: 80
      fixed-delay-ms: 30000
      batch-size: 2000
      rate-limit-per-second: 100
      async:
        core-size: 4
        max-size: 16
        queue-capacity: 100
        keep-alive-seconds: 60
    reactive:
      thread-pool:
        core-size: 30
        max-size: 300
        queue-capacity: 10000
        keep-alive-seconds: 60

# =====================================================
# Metrics
# =====================================================
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        # /metrics is for: JVM / thread pools / custom metrics
        include: health,info,prometheus,metrics
  metrics:
    distribution:
      # Only enable histogram for lock-hold metric (not global),
      # so we can compute p95/p99 from *_bucket while keeping overhead low.
      percentiles-histogram:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: true
      # Histogram bucket lower bound for fine-grained range.
      # Values below 1ms are still counted (not dropped), but won't be over-segmented.
      minimum-expected-value:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: 1ms
      # Histogram bucket upper bound for fine-grained range.
      # Values above 60s are still counted (not dropped), and fall into top bucket(s)
      # instead of creating more detailed buckets at very large durations.
      maximum-expected-value:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: 60s

# =====================================================
# Policy Reprocess Job Configuration
# =====================================================
policy:
  reprocess:
    # 1. producer side
    mark-init-batch-size: 1000

    # 2. cron job side
    scan-interval-ms: 30000
    scan-batch-size: 200
    max-candidates-per-scan: 2000
    scan-lock-ttl-seconds: 900
    rate-limit-per-second: 100

    # listener thread pool: handles PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent trigger
    listener:
      core-size: 1
      max-size: 2
      queue-capacity: 100
      keep-alive-seconds: 60

    # job thread pool: executes per-entity reprocess tasks inside each scan batch
    job:
      core-size: 8
      max-size: 16
      queue-capacity: 32
      keep-alive-seconds: 60

Now I have a solid understanding of the codebase. Let me design the solution:

The `Constants.BusinesCode` is in the common module (`com.fortinet.nac.common.Constants`). Since I can't find the actual source file (it's in a different module that's likely a JAR dependency), I need to figure out a way to add a new error code. 

Wait - looking at the code, all the error codes used are like `Constants.BusinesCode.STATE_MACHINE_DB_PROCESSING_STATE_UPDATE_FAILED`, `Constants.BusinesCode.ENTITY_NOT_FOUND`. Since I can't modify the common module's Constants class directly (it's a separate artifact), I have two options:


1. Use a string-based error in `StatusOr.error(String message)` - I can see `CachedExistingEntityHandler` already does this without a business code
2. Or check if there's a `StatusOr.error(int code, String msg)` variant

Looking at the existing patterns, the cleanest approach is to follow what's already there - using the `StatusOr.error()` method with a business code constant and message, which is the standard pattern throughout the codebase.

I need to figure out where to define the business code constant. The `Constants` class is in the common module as a JAR dependency, so I can't modify it directly. Let me check if there's a local Constants file in this project or if I should define the constant elsewhere.

Since I can't find a local Constants.java, I'll follow the pattern used in `CachedExistingEntityHandler` and just pass the error message directly to `StatusOr.error()` for this new case.

For the implementation, I need to create a configuration class for the policy service with a RestTemplate bean and properties binding, build an HTTP client wrapper to handle the notification calls, add the service URL to the application configuration, and then inject the client into the handler to send notifications after updating the processing state.

The notification should go right after `updateProcessingState` succeeds but before starting the state machine, wrapped in error handling that logs failures and returns an error status if needed. I'll use `postForEntity` to access the HTTP status code and configure a timeout through the request factory.

For the request payload, I'll create a simple DTO with the entity ID, source, and attribute fingerprint pulled from the Entity object. Since `IngestionRequest` lives in `com.fortinet.nac.common.dto`, I'll put the notification request DTO there too.

Now I'm deciding on the package structure for the client itself. The handler is in `consumer/handler`, services are in `service`, and config is in `config`. I'll create a `PolicyNotificationClient` in a new `client` package within the ingestion module, keeping the DTO either as an inner class or in the same package for simplicity.

I'm also thinking through the configuration—I need to add the policy service URL and timeout settings to the application properties, though I should check if there's already a `policy` section I can extend rather than creating a duplicate.

Writing the config class... Writing the notification client... I'm verifying that `StatusOr.error(String)` is the right method to use here, and it looks like it is based on the existing code. Now I need to think about whether using Java records is appropriate for this codebase—the project seems to be using Lombok annotations like `@Data` and `@Builder` on JPA entities, which suggests it might be targeting an older Java version than 16, so I should probably stick with a traditional class instead to be safe. Writing the DTO class... I'm settling on creating a static inner class for the notification DTO to keep things simple and avoid file proliferation. Now I need to set up the configuration and client files, then modify the existing handler and application properties to wire everything together.

server:
  port: 8086

spring:
  application:
    name: ingestion

  redis:
    # host and port will be overridden by Testcontainers automatically
    host: localhost
    port: 6379
    timeout: 15000

    # Redisson-specific configuration
    redisson:
      # Connection pool settings
      #
      # Maximum number of connections in Redis command connection pool.
      # Redisson doesn't distinguish max-idle, only cares about: maximum pool size and minimum keep-alive connections.
      #
      # Connection pool optimized for 24 stream consumers + business requests
      connection-pool-size: 96

      # Minimum number of idle connections to retain in the pool.
      # Even if Redis isn't accessed for some time, keep at least this many connections ready.
      # Helps avoid "cold start latency" — no need to establish a new connection when requests resume.
      #
      # High QPS scenario, pre-warm 24 connections.
      # Keep a batch of connections resident in the pool, aligned with consumer count.
      connection-minimum-idle-size: 24

      # Lettuce shares Pub/Sub and regular connections in the same pool (or a few independent).
      # But Redisson is different.
      #
      # Project currently doesn't use Pub/Sub.
      subscription-connection-pool-size: 8
      subscription-connection-minimum-idle-size: 2

      # Redisson thread model:
      #      nettyThreads:	IO threads
      #      threads:       	Redisson workers (locks/callbacks/scheduling)
      # These two thread pools are global, not per-bean
      #
      # Recommended empirical values:
      #      nettyThreads ≈ CPU * 2
      #      threads ≈ CPU
      netty-threads: 16
      threads: 8

      # Retry settings
      retry-attempts: 5
      retry-interval: 1000

  datasource:
    url: jdbc:mariadb://localhost/bsc?localSocket=/var/lib/mysql/mysql.sock&zeroDateTimeBehavior=convertToNull&useUnicode=true&characterEncoding=UTF-8&CharSet=UTF-8
    username: root
    password: ""
    driver-class-name: org.mariadb.jdbc.Driver

  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: validate
      naming:
        physical-strategy: org.hibernate.boot.model.naming.PhysicalNamingStrategyStandardImpl
    show-sql: false
    database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.MariaDBDialect

logging:
  config: classpath:logback-spring.xml



# =====================================================
# NAC Stream consumer & producer configuration (used by RedisStreamConsumerBase)
# =====================================================
nac:
  stream:
    max-length: 200000            # Maximum number of messages to retain in stream
    trim-enabled: true
    trim-every-n-writes: 1000

    internal:
      poll-timeout-ms: 1000             # to avoid busy poll
      message-batch-size-per-poll: 20   # Too large batches will increase p95/max (worse tail latency).
      redis-consumer-count: 16
      worker-thread-pool-size: 32
    external:
      poll-timeout-ms: 1000             # to avoid busy poll
      message-batch-size-per-poll: 50
      redis-consumer-count: 8
      worker-thread-pool-size: 8

    consume-max-retry: 3              # Max retry attempts on message consumption failure
    create-group-include-history: true  # Include historical messages when creating group during tests
    pending:
      idle-threshold-ms: 10000        # Consider stuck if not ACKed within 10 seconds
      scan-interval-ms: 5000          # Scan pending messages every 5 seconds
      claim-batch-size: 10            # Number of messages to claim per XPENDING batch


# =====================================================
# Ingestion Controller Reactive Netty Config
# =====================================================
ingestion:
    recovery:
      enabled: true
      stale-threshold-seconds: 80
      fixed-delay-ms: 30000
      batch-size: 2000
      rate-limit-per-second: 100
      async:
        core-size: 4
        max-size: 16
        queue-capacity: 100
        keep-alive-seconds: 60
    reactive:
      thread-pool:
        core-size: 30
        max-size: 300
        queue-capacity: 10000
        keep-alive-seconds: 60

# =====================================================
# Metrics
# =====================================================
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        # /metrics is for: JVM / thread pools / custom metrics
        include: health,info,prometheus,metrics
  metrics:
    distribution:
      # Only enable histogram for lock-hold metric (not global),
      # so we can compute p95/p99 from *_bucket while keeping overhead low.
      percentiles-histogram:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: true
      # Histogram bucket lower bound for fine-grained range.
      # Values below 1ms are still counted (not dropped), but won't be over-segmented.
      minimum-expected-value:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: 1ms
      # Histogram bucket upper bound for fine-grained range.
      # Values above 60s are still counted (not dropped), and fall into top bucket(s)
      # instead of creating more detailed buckets at very large durations.
      maximum-expected-value:
        policy_reproc_lock_hold_seconds: 60s

# =====================================================
# Policy Reprocess Job Configuration
# =====================================================
policy:
  reprocess:
    # 1. producer side
    mark-init-batch-size: 1000

    # 2. cron job side
    scan-interval-ms: 30000
    scan-batch-size: 200
    max-candidates-per-scan: 2000
    scan-lock-ttl-seconds: 900
    rate-limit-per-second: 100

    # listener thread pool: handles PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent trigger
    listener:
      core-size: 1
      max-size: 2
      queue-capacity: 100
      keep-alive-seconds: 60

    # job thread pool: executes per-entity reprocess tasks inside each scan batch
    job:
      core-size: 8
      max-size: 16
      queue-capacity: 32
      keep-alive-seconds: 60
